dict.string: Eidos reads your next calendar event so the What Now flow can offer a built-in stop signal ("you have 23 minutes before X"). Event data never leaves your iPhone.
Eidos uses the camera for the Look flow — point at a cluttered scene and Gemma 4 narrates a 3-step plan, on-device. Images never leave your iPhone.
Eidos uses your contacts to understand who you mean when you reference a person by name in journal entries. Contact data never leaves your iPhone.
Reserved for v2 — sensory-regulation biofeedback via HRV. Not used in v1. Data never leaves your iPhone.
Eidos doesn't write to HealthKit. This permission is required by iOS when requesting any health access.
Reserved for v2. Not used in v1.
Reserved for v2 — location-aware nudges. Not used in v1.
Eidos transcribes your voice on-device so you can journal, ground, and ask hands-free. Audio never leaves your iPhone.
Eidos reads photos you explicitly pick so Gemma 4 can describe the scene for a step-by-step plan, on-device. Images never leave your iPhone.
Eidos may create a reminder from a journal entry on request. Reminder data never leaves your iPhone.
Eidos uses Apple's on-device speech recognition to turn your voice into text. Your speech is never sent to a server.
